questions.question: Explain the importance of political institutions in a democratic system.
questions.answer: Political institutions ensure decisions are made through rules and procedures and involve multiple authorities in governance.

questions.question: Explain how major policy decisions are taken in India with reference to the Mandal Commission case.
questions.answer: The Cabinet approved the Mandal Commission recommendation, Parliament debated it and the government issued an official order to implement reservation.

questions.question: Discuss the functions of Parliament in maintaining democratic governance.
questions.answer: Parliament performs law making, financial control and represents public concerns through debates.

questions.question: Explain why the Lok Sabha is considered more powerful than the Rajya Sabha.
questions.answer: Lok Sabha controls the Council of Ministers and has greater authority over financial matters and money bills.

questions.question: Explain how Parliament exercises control over the executive.
questions.answer: Parliament controls the executive through question hour, debates, financial control and no-confidence motions.

questions.question: Discuss the significance of the bicameral legislature in India.
questions.answer: The bicameral system ensures balanced representation of people and states and improves legislative scrutiny.

questions.question: Explain the structure and powers of the Indian Parliament.
questions.answer: The Parliament consists of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha and is responsible for law making and financial control.

questions.question: Explain the difference between political executive and permanent executive.
questions.answer: Political executives are elected leaders responsible for policy decisions while permanent executives are civil servants responsible for administration.

questions.question: Explain the powers and responsibilities of the Prime Minister.
questions.answer: The Prime Minister leads the Cabinet, coordinates ministries and plays a key role in policy making.

questions.question: Discuss the role of the Council of Ministers in governance.
questions.answer: The Council of Ministers assists the Prime Minister in decision making and administration.

questions.question: Explain the constitutional position of the President of India.
questions.answer: The President is the head of the state and acts according to the advice of the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: Explain how the President of India is elected.
questions.answer: The President is elected by an electoral college consisting of elected MPs and MLAs.

questions.question: Discuss the role of civil servants in public administration.
questions.answer: Civil servants assist ministers in implementing policies and managing administration efficiently.

questions.question: Explain the concept of judiciary in India.
questions.answer: The judiciary is the system of courts responsible for interpreting laws and ensuring justice.

questions.question: Explain the structure of the Indian judiciary.
questions.answer: The Indian judiciary consists of the Supreme Court, High Courts and lower courts.

questions.question: Explain the importance of judicial independence.
questions.answer: Judicial independence ensures fair judgments without political influence.

questions.question: Explain judicial review and its significance.
questions.answer: Judicial review allows courts to examine laws and declare them unconstitutional if they violate the Constitution.

questions.question: Explain Public Interest Litigation and its importance.
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to approach courts for matters affecting public interest.

questions.question: Explain how courts settle disputes in a democratic society.
questions.answer: Courts interpret laws and provide judgments to resolve disputes between individuals, organizations and government.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Supreme Court in India.
questions.answer: The Supreme Court is the highest court and protects the Constitution and citizens' rights.

questions.question: Explain how institutions prevent misuse of power.
questions.answer: Institutions distribute power among different authorities and ensure decisions follow procedures.

questions.question: Explain the concept of checks and balances among institutions.
questions.answer: Checks and balances ensure that no single institution becomes too powerful in governance.

questions.question: Explain the importance of debates and discussions in Parliament.
questions.answer: Debates help representatives discuss policies and hold the government accountable.

questions.question: Explain why rules and procedures are important in decision making.
questions.answer: Rules ensure transparency, fairness and accountability in governance.

questions.question: Explain how institutions strengthen democracy.
questions.answer: Institutions ensure fair decision making, distribute power and maintain accountability in governance.
